title: sideritis syriaca subsp. catillaris
intro: Sideritis syriaca subsp. catillaris, also known as Greek mountain tea, is a plant species native to the Mediterranean region, belonging to the genus Sideritis. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Sideritis syriaca subsp. catillaris include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting potential antiviral and antibacterial activities. The plant is typically prepared as an herbal tea by infusing its leaves in hot water, and it can also be used in the form of capsules, tinctures, and essential oils.
